repo.security_posture: private network app; mTLS to paired bridges; Android Keystore for client cert + private keys; never store private key bytes outside Keystore

parent_project.note: Track B of CubeOS — MeshSat. Companion to the meshsat/ Bridge (project 27); peers with meshsat/ and meshsat-hub/ in the 3-node mesh.

task.title: DirectorySyncService + ContactCardImporter
task.prompt: FIRST: read spec/002-directory-sync/{requirements.md, design.md, acceptance/directory-sync.feature}. SECOND: add service/DirectorySyncService.kt (alongside existing GatewayService.kt + TransportRegistry.kt) and crypto/ContactCardImporter.kt (alongside KeyBundleImporter.kt — same shape: sealed class inline, explicit "BC" provider per Article C-II). Per-bridge poll + Ed25519 verify against bridge_trust.pubkey + Room transaction apply + MQTT-trigger subscriber. Tests at FLAT layout per Article C-VIII.

slo_targets._notes: placeholder — refine during task execution based on profiling

observability._notes: Placeholder block for T-002. Replace with concrete metric names as code lands. If this task does NOT need metrics (e.g. CI/build task), replace this block with `no_observability_required: "<reason>"` instead.

requirements.body: The system shall add `service/DirectorySyncService.kt` alongside the existing `service/GatewayService.kt` and `service/TransportRegistry.kt`.
When the service is started, the system shall iterate every paired bridge in `bridge_trust` and issue a GET https://<bridge>/api/directory/snapshot?since=<last_sync_at>.
When the service receives a snapshot response, the system shall verify the Ed25519 signature against the stored `bridge_trust.pubkey` for that bridge.
If the snapshot signature verification fails, then the system shall log a WARN event to AuditLog and not apply the snapshot.
When verification succeeds, the system shall apply the snapshot inside a single Room transaction touching only the `paired_bridge_*` tables for that bridge_hash.
While running, the system shall subscribe to the MQTT topic `meshsat/bridge/{bridge_hash}/directory/changed` and trigger an immediate snapshot pull within 5 seconds of receiving any event.
When the operator scans a contact-card QR, the system shall call `crypto/ContactCardImporter.kt` to verify the embedded signature (Ed25519, via "BC" provider per Article C-II) before persisting.
If contact-card signature verification fails, then the system shall surface an inline error in the UI and not persist.

The system shall implement `crypto/ContactCardImporter.kt` following the same shape as the shipped `crypto/KeyBundleImporter.kt` — sealed-class `ImportResult` inline, explicit `"BC"` provider, no JNI.
The system shall NOT modify or replace `crypto/KeyBundleImporter.kt` while adding `ContactCardImporter.kt`.

The system shall add a JVM-runnable test `app/src/test/java/com/cubeos/meshsat/DirectorySyncServiceTest.kt` covering: successful sync, signature failure, MQTT-trigger pull, stale-data banner threshold (flat layout per Article C-VIII).

The system shall add a JVM-runnable test `app/src/test/java/com/cubeos/meshsat/ContactCardImporterTest.kt` covering: valid QR import, tampered-sig rejection, malformed-payload rejection (flat layout per Article C-VIII).

acceptance.scenario: First sync from bridge populates paired_bridge_* tables
Signature mismatch logs WARN and does not apply
MQTT directory/changed event triggers immediate pull
PeopleScreen lists contacts; tap navigates to ContactDetailScreen
Adding ContactCardImporter does not modify KeyBundleImporter

family_invariants_relevant_to_this_repo.short: API containers NEVER touch host services directly — all via HAL REST
Schema migrations file is append-only; never edit existing entries
CLAUDE.md and .claude/ NEVER committed; spec-kit artefacts ARE committed
Parallel-dev waves use merge/<feature_id> branches + 1 MR per feature
All commits use Kyriakos Papadopoulos / ncpjfuzl@mxmx.email via -c flags
